bonuses.leadin: In a painting in this chapel, a woman in the foreground pours from a jug as a reclining woman behind her stares at a baby. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this chapel, where that painting, The Nativity of Mary, sits next to a scene of Joachim’s (“JO-uh-kim’s”) expulsion from the temple. In this chapel, 8 scenes from the life of Mary face 8 scenes of the life of John the Baptist.

The frescoes in the Tornabuoni Chapel in the Santa Maria Novella were painted by the workshop of this Florentine master of the late 15th century, who depicted a man with a bulbous nose in An Old Man and his Grandson.
Some work on the frescoes in the Tornabuoni Chapel may have been done by this then-thirteen-year-old student of Ghirlandaio. Decades later, this man painted the fresco The Last Judgment.
answers: Domenico Ghirlandaio (“gheer-lahn-DYE-oh”) [or Domenico di Tommaso Curradi di Doffo Bigordi]
Michelangelo [or Michelangelo Buonarroti; or Michelangelo di Lodovico Buonarroti Simoni]
answers_sanitized: Domenico Ghirlandaio ("gheer-lahn-DYE-oh") [or Domenico di Tommaso Curradi di Doffo Bigordi]
Michelangelo [or Michelangelo Buonarroti; or Michelangelo di Lodovico Buonarroti Simoni]
